summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates unbounded resource consumption from List, RangeScan, and Read operations without iteration count limits. The exploit sends requests covering the entire keyspace, causing CPU, memory, and I/O exhaustion.
expected_security_impact: Running the exploit should cause high CPU usage (idle < 20%), high memory usage (> 80%), or multiple worker processes due to unbounded iteration over the entire keyspace.
actual_observed_result: PoC not executed during this session. Manual execution required via ./reproduction/run.sh.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks CPU idle percentage and memory usage percentage. If CPU idle < 20% or memory used > 80%, the vulnerability is confirmed. Additional check for process count > 10 indicates multiple worker processes.
evidence_produced_by_run: PoC artifacts prepared but not executed
Dockerfile, exploit.sh, verify.sh, setup.sh, run.sh created
Expected outcome: high resource consumption indicators
limitations_or_blockers: PoC requires Docker execution which was not performed during this automated session
Requires sufficient test data (10000 keys) to demonstrate impact
Requires monitoring tools (top, free) in Alpine container

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub or uses local repo, builds the Docker image, and runs exploitation
